honesty: The K=1 conv proxy is SRAM-STAGING-bound: its working set (output N*M elements) stays below the ~8.2M residency-cliff knee (on-chip SRAM capacity) for every M that compiles (M<=1024; M>=2048 fails), so eff_BW reflects on-chip throughput and RISES with transfer size (9.6 -> 44.4 GB/s, +24% at the last step) instead of converging to a DRAM plateau. The proxy never reaches the DRAM regime, so it CANNOT validate the kv_append DRAM bandwidth. kv_append stays ANALYTIC on M2's measured streaming BW (24.2 GB/s); the earlier single-point 'M=256 ~ M2' match was coincidental.

verdict.note_spill_floor: the residency-cliff spill floor (M=1 GEMV streaming weights from DRAM) is the only DRAM-BOUND on-card BW datapoint; same order as M2.

kv_append_basis: kv_append stays analytic = kv_bytes / M2 measured DRAM streaming BW (24.2 GB/s, from the decode weight-stream wall). This SPIKE did NOT add independent validation: the on-card proxy could not be pushed into the DRAM regime within the compile envelope (working set < SRAM knee).
